title: Indigenous Health Data Sovereignty And Service Access Gaps In Settler-Colonial States
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring indigenous health service access gaps relative to non-indigenous populations, linking indigenous-identifier data, health-service utilization data, and community-health-facility data at sub-national scale while respecting indigenous data sovereignty principles.
why_it_matters: Indigenous populations have life expectancy 5-20 years lower than non-indigenous populations in every country where data exists. Yet indigenous identifiers are absent from most national health information systems, and where they exist, data sovereignty principles demand indigenous governance of that data. Measuring without owning is the challenge.

success_metrics.target: Every data use respects CARE principles and indigenous data governance.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms methodology against sovereignty standards.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
